headlines.title: Ukraine Hits St. Petersburg With Massive Wave of Drones
headlines.description: A wave of Ukrainian drones targeted sites in St. Petersburg where Russian President Vladimir Putin is kicking off a closely-watched international summit. It comes after Ukraine suffered one of the worst onslaughts of the war. NBC’s Keir Simmons reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: House votes to rebuke Trump over war with Iran
headlines.description: The House offered a rare rebuke to President Donald Trump, passing a Democratic-led measure to end his war with Iran over objections from Republican leadership.

headlines.title: Steve Kornacki breaks down California's ‘cliffhanger’ and the first Trump-backed candidate loss
headlines.description: NBC News Chief Data Analyst Steve Kornacki joins Meet the Press NOW to break down the results of primary races across the nation and what Californians can expect on the ballot in the general election come November. KNBC-TV Political Reporter Conan Nolan analyzes the Los Angeles mayoral race.

headlines.title: In Iowa primary for governor, Trump’s pick concedes to MAHA-backed candidate
headlines.description: Rep. Randy Feenstra conceded to businessman Zach Lahn in Iowa’s Republican primary for governor as party voters rejected President Donald Trump’s late endorsement in the race.

headlines.title: SpaceX files for IPO in move that could make Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire
headlines.description: SpaceX has filed for an initial public offering at a valuation of $1.77 trillion that could make founder and CEO Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire. NBC News' Allie Canal has more.

headlines.title: CIA officer arrested with gold bars worked on Pentagon's nuclear program, sources say
headlines.description: David Rush, a CIA officer, was arrested after $40 million in gold bars was found at his home. Sources say Rush worked as a liaison to the Pentagon for a sensitive nuclear submarine program. NBC News' Courtney Kube explains.

headlines.title: CBS News’ Bari Weiss addresses ‘unfortunate’ firing of Scott Pelley
headlines.description: CBS News Editor-in-Chief Bari Weiss, speaking to network employees Wednesday about the firing of “60 Minutes” correspondent Scott Pelley, said newsroom leaders could not “find a way back” with the veteran journalist — a comment he flatly rejected in a statement of his own

headlines.title: Florida pastor and son’s $8 million Covid fraud case comes to bizarre end
headlines.description: It was one of the strangest Covid fraud cases brought by the Justice Department, with the kind of wild details that seemed ripped from a Hollywood script

headlines.title: Decision to allow three boys convicted of rape to walk free sparks fury and debate in U.K.
headlines.description: LONDON — A judge’s decision to spare three teenage boys found guilty of rape at knifepoint and other serious sexual offenses from a custodial sentence has sparked outrage across the U.K.

headlines.title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final
headlines.description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

headlines.title: Senate Republicans move forward with their big immigration bill after internal clashes: From the Politics Desk
headlines.description: Sahil Kapur previews the road ahead for congressional Republicans’ next reconciliation bill.

headlines.title: Man found with zip ties and duct tape charged with assaulting 5 women at UCLA
headlines.description: A man was charged Wednesday with assaulting five women in less than an hour at the University of California Los Angeles last month, officials said
